messages.content: Echo, run heartbeat check — session type: `heartbeat`, domain: `task_staleness`. Timestamp: 2026/02/22 (Sun) 17:29 SAST.

## 💓 Heartbeat — Task Staleness Audit
**Session ID:** `heartbeat_20260222_1729`
**Timestamp:** 2026/02/22 17:29 SAST
**Agent:** Echo (main)

---

### 🗂️ Stale Task Scan Results

Scanning open task queue against last-activity timestamps...

| Task ID | Description | Last Touch | Age | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `TASK-041` | Browser automation fallback config | 2026/02/19 | **3d** | ⚠️ Stale |
| `TASK-038` | CodexAI onboarding flow QA pass | 2026/02/20 | **2d** | ⚠️ Stale |
| `TASK-044` | TrustAlign sub-agent scope doc | 2026/02/21 | 1d | 🟡 Aging |
| `TASK-047` | Planner-executor model spec review | 2026/02/22 08:05 | <12h | ✅ Fresh |
| `TASK-049` | File ops integrity check | 2026/02/22 13:04 | <5h | ✅ Fresh |

**Summary:** 2 stale tasks (>48h untouched), 1 aging, 2 fresh.

> ⚠️ `TASK-041` is highest priority — browser issues flagged during Week 1 remain unresolved.
What's the blocker on TASK-041? And why is TASK-038 stale — CodexAI went live this week, I'd expect that to be wrapped up.

### 🔍 Blocker Detail — TASK-041 & TASK-038

---

#### `TASK-041` — Browser Automation Fallback Config
**Last owner:** worker sub-agent
**Blocker type:** Environment dependency

The browser issues that surfaced around 2026/02/19 were traced to a Playwright session leak in the worker's headless Chrome pool. The fix requires a config change to `worker/browser_pool.yaml` — specifically the `max_sessions` and `idle_timeout_ms` parameters. However, the updated config template was pending sign-off on the circuit breaker thresholds we reviewed in the heartbeat session at 23:47 on 2026/02/21.

That sign-off was never recorded as complete. **Task is blocked on a dependency closure that got lost between sessions.**

---

#### `TASK-038` — CodexAI Onboarding Flow QA Pass
**Last owner:** Echo (me)
**Blocker type:** Scope drift / deprioritisation

CodexAI went live with first users on 2026/02/20, but the QA pass got deprioritised when the social engagement pipeline work (engage-x) pulled attention on 2026/02/20 afternoon. The onboarding flow was considered "good enough" for early users but the formal QA checklist — 14 items — is only ~60% complete.

**Risk:** With real users now in the funnel, any onboarding friction compounds daily. This should not stay stale.

---

**Root cause pattern:** Both tasks lost momentum during context switches tied to the sub-agent buildout week (TrustAlign creation, engage-x pipeline). Classic Week 1 sprawl.

OK. Close out the circuit breaker dependency for TASK-041 — I'm signing off on the thresholds we set Friday night. And escalate TASK-038 to P1, assign it to worker for a QA sweep first thing tomorrow. Log both updates.

### ✅ Task Updates Logged
**Timestamp:** 2026/02/22 17:34 SAST
**Authorised by:** Alex

---

#### `TASK-041` — Dependency Closure

```
[UPDATE] TASK-041
Blocker: circuit_breaker_threshold_signoff → RESOLVED
Authority: Alex (verbal, 2026/02/22 17:34 SAST)
Ref session: heartbeat_20260221_2347 (thresholds discussed)
Next action: worker to apply browser_pool.yaml patch
  - max_sessions: 6
  - idle_timeout_ms: 45000
  - breaker_threshold: 3 consecutive failures
Status: UNBLOCKED → assigned to worker, priority P2

```

---

#### `TASK-038` — Escalation to P1

```
[UPDATE] TASK-038
Priority: P3 → P1
Assigned to: worker sub-agent
Scheduled start: 2026/02/23 08:00 SAST
Scope: Complete remaining 6/14 QA checklist items
  - Error state handling (steps 9–11)
  - Mobile viewport rendering (steps 12–13)
  - Session persistence on reload (step 14)
Risk note: CodexAI active users = ~12 as of today
Status: ESCALATED → queued for worker morning sweep
Target: 2026/02/23 12:00 SAST
```

---

🔔 worker sub-agent notified via internal task bus. Both updates written to `task_log_20260222.jsonl`.
Good. What's the broader lesson here for task hygiene? We can't keep losing context on blockers between sessions.

### 📌 Lesson — Cross-Session Blocker Hygiene

This week exposed a structural gap: **blockers that depend on decisions made in one session aren't reliably tracked into the next session's context.** TASK-041 is the clearest example — the circuit breaker sign-off happened at 23:47 on Friday, but by Saturday morning that dependency wasn't surfaced in any active queue.

Here's the pattern I'm flagging for the self-improvement review process we started this morning (`main_session_20260222_0805`):

---

#### 🔁 Root Cause
When a task is blocked on a **human decision** (not a technical dependency), it exits the active worker queue and silently ages. There's no polling mechanism to re-surface it unless a heartbeat explicitly scans for stale items — which is exactly what this session is for, but only if heartbeats run frequently enough.

#### 🛠️ Proposed Fix — Three-Layer Blocker Protocol

1. **Blocker tagging at time of block** — Any task moved to `BLOCKED` state must have a `blocker_type` and `blocker_owner` field set. Human-owned blockers get a 24h TTL before auto-escalation to heartbeat queue.

2. **Session handoff stubs** — At the close of any session where a decision is *deferred*, Echo generates a one-line stub in `pending_decisions.md`. This survived the Friday night session gap that cost us 3 days on TASK-041.

3. **Heartbeat staleness threshold = 36h** — Tasks untouched for 36h surface automatically in the next heartbeat. Current implicit threshold was somewhere around 72h based on this scan.

---

I'll draft this as a formal hygiene rule for the Week 1 → Week 2 retrospective. The planner-executor model we documented this week is the right skeleton — it just needs explicit blocker lifecycle hooks added.

> TL;DR: Decisions deferred between sessions need a physical artifact. Memory alone doesn't survive context switches.
Draft that hygiene rule now and add it to the system docs.

### 📝 Drafting — Cross-Session Blocker Hygiene Rule
**Target file:** `docs/system/task_hygiene.md`
**Section:** Blocker Lifecycle Protocol

---

Writing to system docs now...

```markdown
## Rule: Cross-Session Blocker Lifecycle Protocol
**Added:** 2026/02/22 — Echo (heartbeat_20260222_1729)
**Review by:** 2026/03/01

### Scope
Applies to all tasks in the OpenClaw task bus that enter a BLOCKED state,
regardless of sub-agent ownership.

### Protocol

**1. Blocker Registration (at time of block)**
Every BLOCKED task must record:
- `blocker_type`: one of `human_decision | technical | external | dependency`
- `blocker_owner`: agent ID or "alex"
- `blocker_created_at`: ISO timestamp (SAST)
- `blocker_ttl_hours`: default 24h for human_decision, 48h for technical

**2. Pending Decision Stub**
If a session ends with any deferred human decision, Echo appends to
`docs/pending_decisions.md`:
  > [DATE] [TASK-ID] — [one-line decision description] — Owner: Alex

This file is read at the start of every heartbeat session.

**3. Heartbeat Staleness Threshold**
- Tasks untouched for **36 hours** auto-surface in the next heartbeat scan.
- Previous implicit threshold (~72h) is formally retired as of this rule.

**4. Blocker Closure**
When a blocker is resolved (verbally or in-session), the resolving agent must:
- Record authority (who resolved, timestamp, ref session ID)
- Update task status from BLOCKED → UNBLOCKED
- Assign next action owner before closing the update

### Origin
Derived from TASK-041 postmortem (2026/02/22). Circuit breaker sign-off
made in heartbeat_20260221_2347 was not propagated, causing 3-day stall.
See also: error_patterns review in main_session_20260222_0805.
```

---

✅ **Written to:** `docs/system/task_hygiene.md`
✅ **Appended to:** `docs/pending_decisions.md` (cleared — no open deferred decisions as of this session)
✅ **Logged in:** `heartbeat_20260222_1729` session record

This rule will be included in the Week 1 → Week 2 retrospective brief. Want me to flag it for TrustAlign review as an alignment-adjacent process change?
